page: Microsoft Azure D64_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv4 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, featuring 64 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped from 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz and includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 35 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512F, and AVX512_VNNI.

The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual CPU. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage must be attached externally, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64_v4 exhibits strong multi-core CPU performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core score of 59622.57 and a PassMark CPU extended instructions test score of 55022.71. Most other metrics, including single-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, fall within the average range. However, the server shows performance limitations in large language model text generation, scoring in the weak to poor categories for both 135M and 7B parameter models.

Given its balanced resource allocation and lack of bundled local storage, the D64_v4 offers standard cost efficiency for general-purpose enterprise workloads that require high multi-core processing capacity without local disk dependencies. It is suitable for multi-threaded applications, web serving, software compilation, and database operations that can leverage its 64 vCPUs and 256 GB of memory.
description: Microsoft Azure D64_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs (32 physical cores) running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses and operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core CPU performance, particularly in stress-ng and extended instruction tests, while single-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations remain average. A notable tradeoff is its weak to poor performance in LLM text generation tasks. This server is best suited for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, software compilation, and general-purpose web serving that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64_v4 is a general-purpose VM featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 256 GB RAM. It offers strong multi-core performance but lacks local storage and has weak LLM text generation speeds.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance. It has no local storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and strong multi-core processing capabilities for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB RAM
Strong multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and extended instruction benchmarks
No bundled local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses
Average performance across memory bandwidth, database operations, and single-core tasks
Weak performance in large language model text generation workloads
Well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and web serving
